title: Barret Zoph is out at OpenAI again after just five months
summary: Barret Zoph has left OpenAI after just five months as head of enterprise AI sales, only months after returning from co-founding Thinking Machines Lab with former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati. His departure is notable given OpenAI's stated focus on enterprise sales as a key revenue driver ahead of its planned IPO.
content:
Five months after returning to OpenAI, Barret Zoph - the company's head of enterprise AI sales - has departed, The Verge has learned. 
Zoph returned to OpenAI in mid-January after a stint as co-founder and CTO of Thinking Machines Lab, the competing AI company founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati. Shortly after Zoph returned to OpenAI, the company said he would lead its push into enterprise - a significant role at OpenAI, since in recent months it had vowed to stop chasing so-called "side quests" and focus on key revenue drivers like enterprise and coding ahead of its planned IPO. 
OpenAI confirmed to The Verge that Zoph will be depart …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Barret Zoph 在仅五个月后再次离开 OpenAI
zh.summary: Barret Zoph 在担任企业 AI 销售负责人五个月后离开 OpenAI，仅几个月前他从与前 OpenAI CTO Mira Murati 共同创办的 Thinking Machines Lab 回归。考虑到 OpenAI 声称企业销售是 IPO 前的关键收入驱动力，他的离职尤其值得关注。

title: Telegram ban in India sparks a rush to VPNs, rival apps
summary: India's ban on Telegram is driving users toward VPNs and alternative messaging apps, affecting millions of users. Telegram contends that banning the entire platform is excessive and argues for targeted content moderation instead. This highlights the tension between government content regulation and platform freedom.
content: Telegram argues India should block specific content, not an entire platform used by millions.

zh.title: 印度禁用Telegram引发用户争相使用VPN和竞争应用
zh.summary: 印度禁用Telegram促使用户转向VPN和替代即时通讯应用，影响数百万用户。Telegram辩称完全禁用该平台过于极端，主张进行有针对性的内容管制。这凸显了政府内容监管与平台自由之间的紧张关系。

title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M
summary: Elastic has agreed to acquire DeductiveAI, an AI-powered bug detection and resolution startup, for up to $85 million. Founded just three years ago and backed by Craft Ventures, DeductiveAI represents Elastic's investment in AI-driven software quality tools. This acquisition signals growing demand for automated bug detection in development workflows.
content: DeductiveAI, a startup that uses AI to catch and resolve bugs in software, was founded just three years ago.

zh.title: 消息来源：Elastic同意以最高8500万美元收购CRV支持的DeductiveAI
zh.summary: Elastic已同意收购DeductiveAI，这是一家AI驱动的bug检测和解决方案初创公司，收购价格最高为8500万美元。DeductiveAI成立于三年前，由Craft Ventures支持，代表Elastic在AI驱动的软件质量工具方面的投资。此次收购表明开发工作流中对自动化bug检测的需求不断增长。

title: A bold satellite rescue mission came together in record time, but will it work?
summary: A satellite rescue mission was organized in record time, representing an ambitious attempt to recover a disabled satellite. Officials view the effort itself as a major achievement, even before determining whether the technical rescue will succeed. This showcases rapid coordination and planning in space operations.
content: "I consider this a success already, just from the fact that we're even going to try this."

zh.title: 大胆的卫星救援任务以创纪录的时间组织完成，但会成功吗？
zh.summary: 卫星救援任务以创纪录的速度组织完成，代表了一次雄心勃勃的尝试来修复一颗故障卫星。官员们认为这一努力本身就是一项重大成就，即使在确定技术救援是否会成功之前也是如此。这展示了太空操作中的快速协调和规划。
